LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS THERESA MOORE AND SCOTT GUSTAFSON'S MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY ISSUE SANCTIONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT MARY T. MOORE SHALL, WITHIN 5 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER, COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S JUNE 14, 2019 BY PROVIDING COMPLETE, CODE-COMPLIANT, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO ALL DISCOVERY REQUESTS, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS. DEFENDANT IS ADMONISHED THAT ANY FURTHER FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDERS COULD RESULT IN THE ENTRY OF ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Mtn For Evidentiary Issue Sanctions Or In The Alt For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS THERESA MOORE AND SCOTT GUSTAFSON'S Motion For Evidentiary Issue Sanctions Or In The Alternative For Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: As to PLAINTIFFS THERESA MOORE AND SCOTT GUSTAFSON'S Motion For Evidentiary Issue Sanctions Or In The Alternative For Terminating Sanctions, the report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiffs' motion for issue and evidentiary sanctions or in the alternative for terminating sanctions is denied without prejudice. Defendant Mary T. Moore shall, within 5 days of entry of the court's order, comply with the Court's June 14, 2019 by providing complete, Code-compliant, verified responses to all discovery requests, without objections. Defendant is admonished that any further failure to comply with the Court's discovery orders could result in the entry of issue, evidentiary, or terminating sanctions.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)
